subject: Biology
title: Stem Cells: The Blank Slate
description: A quick, practical guide to Stem Cells: The Blank Slate.

hook.text: Stem cells can become almost any cell type. They are biology's wild cards.

buildup.text: Embryonic stem cells are pluripotent—they can turn into muscle, nerve, bone, or blood.

discovery.text: In 2006, Yamanaka showed adult cells could be reprogrammed back into stem cells.

twist.text: These induced stem cells could grow replacement organs without needing embryos.

climax.text: Stem cell therapies already treat blood cancers through bone marrow transplants.

punchline.text: One cell type with infinite potential.

quiz.question: What did Yamanaka demonstrate in 2006?
options: Adult cells can be reprogrammed into stem cells
Stem cells can only come from embryos
